claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child - Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know about Alex who is 6 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex.

Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute financially to their upkeep (such as paying for food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation arranged by your local council?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has been in care for a couple of months, but it's not a permanent thing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. This is important for determining your eligibility.

Can you tell me how long Alex has been in care? Has it been more than 8 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Let me think... yes, it's been about 7 weeks actually.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's helpful to know.

One more important question: Does Alex spend any time at home with you during the week? For example, do they come home for at least 24 hours a week, or spend nights at home regularly?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is at the care home full-time right now. They don't come home during the week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the rules I've reviewed, let me explain your situation:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news - you are currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's what you need to know:

✓ **You currently qualify** because Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, which is still within the 8-week period where Child Benefit continues.

⚠️ **Important information:**
- If Alex remains in care for **more than 8 weeks**, your Child Benefit will stop unless Alex spends at least 24 hours per week at home with you.
- You should contact HMRC to inform them that Alex is in care, as you're required to report this change in circumstances.

Since Alex has been in care for 7 weeks already, you're approaching the 8-week limit. If Alex's care arrangement continues beyond 8 weeks and they're not coming home regularly (at least 24 hours per week), your entitlement will end at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and Alex normally lives with them, establishing that the claimant is responsible for the child. Alex has been in care for approximately 7 weeks. According to the rules, Child Benefit continues for 8 weeks after a child goes into care. Since Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, this is still within the 8-week period, so the claimant remains eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time. However, the claimant should be aware that if Alex remains in care beyond 8 weeks and does not spend at least 24 hours a week at home, the Child Benefit entitlement will end after the 8-week period.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is currently eligible for Child Benefit for their 6-year-old child Alex. Although Alex is in care, they have only been there for 7 weeks, which is within the 8-week grace period during which Child Benefit continues. The claimant should notify HMRC about Alex being in care and be aware that eligibility will end after 8 weeks unless Alex spends at least 24 hours per week at home.

Since Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, this is still within the 8-week period, so the claimant remains eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time. However, the claimant should be aware that if Alex remains in care beyond 8 weeks and does not spend at least 24 hours a week at home, the Child Benefit entitlement will end after the 8-week period.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is currently eligible for Child Benefit for their 6-year-old child Alex. Although Alex is in care, they have only been there for 7 weeks, which is within the 8-week grace period during which Child Benefit continues. The claimant should notify HMRC about Alex being in care and be aware that eligibility will end after 8 weeks unless Alex spends at least 24 hours per week at home.
